steps.title: Identify the structure of the coordinated verbs
steps.content: The sentence coordinates two actions with “and”: “merchants exported wheat, gold, and salt and ______ other valuable commodities.” Two verbs joined by “and” with the same subject must use the same grammatical form. The first verb, “exported,” is in past tense.

steps.title: Apply the parallel structure rule
steps.content: ”Imported” (D) provides a past tense verb matching “exported,” which is required for parallel structure in coordinated verbs. Choices A, B, and C use different verb forms that don't match the past tense structure. That matches choice D — D is correct.
